overall settings.title: Infestation
overall settings.description:
After a disastrous lab experiment, Florida has been overrun with mutant rodents. Especially dangerous ones are further south. Vacationers and residents have fled. Many retired folks are missing.

Hack 'n slash dueling where you {lime|level up}.

items.name: Distance
items.description: Measure of how far away the combatants are, *Theater of the Mind* (no tactical grid). Melee distance is 1 unless there's e.g. a long spear with reach 2.

items.name: Energy
items.description:
Required for fighting. A unit is 100 Kilojoules.
Restore by levelling up, or travel to the *Maintenance Building* and click on the *Recharging Station*.
Limit is 10 + 4 * **XL,** plus any special bonuses. Record the bonuses by editing this description:

- No special bonuses yet

items.name: Funding
items.description: Required for repairs, so don't be too reckless or there might not be enough to complete the mission.

items.name: Pool of Water
items.description: Can be used to extinguish flames.

items.name: Recharging Station
items.description: Click on *Energy* to power up the robot.

locations.name: Baby Mice Area
locations.description: The weakest of the rodents can be found here, but the area is alarmingly close to the maintenance building. Populations must be controlled so they don't spread further.

locations.name: Highway
locations.description: Advanced state of disrepair, though it beats trudging through swampland. Provides travel deeper south, for a higher degree of difficulty than the areas near the Maintenance Building.

locations.name: Maintenance Building
locations.description: An outpost in northern Florida set up to contain the infestation. Engineers assess intel and perform upgrades and repairs.

characters.name: 🐁Baby Mouse
characters.description: Mutant, rabid and green-eyed. Low HP but can still bite.

characters.name: 🐰Bad Hare Day
characters.description:
Red-eyed monster that never stops hopping up and down, up and down.

- Set difficulty to *Hard*.
- On victory, mark the boss as [Defeated](/play/attrib/3/char/5) and add **250 XP**.

characters.name: 🐭Full-grown Mouse
characters.description: Okay, it's still kind of cute, despite the toxic green eyes.

characters.name: 🤖 Herbert 2100B
characters.description:
Semi-autonomous robot, the player character.
Fight initiator—always gets the first turn.
Does not get diseases from being bitten, but has a limited amount of power.
Battle testing allows engineers to improve the technology.

characters.name: 👴Old Fart Supreme
characters.description:
Wearing a nametag with "Gerry" written on it.

After his defeat, he can be restored to normal through medical procedures. Whether the process improves cantankerousness is doubtful though.

characters.name: 🐇Tainted Bunny
characters.description: Fur is greenish and sticky, with a fuzzy white tail.

attribs.name: Action Speed
attribs.description: Higher numbers increase your chance of landing hits before they can dodge.

attribs.name: Boost
attribs.description: Gets set by **Surge** for 1 round. It goes away even if the enemy doesn't choose to attack in that round.

attribs.name: Defeated
attribs.description: An enemy with this attribute is unique, so after victory, set to true.

attribs.name: DoT Power
attribs.description: Multiplier to damage on each turn

attribs.name: DoT Turns
attribs.description: How many remaining turns does the damage apply for.

attribs.name: Force
attribs.description: How much power is available to hit or move things. Equates to Strength for living beings.

attribs.name: HP
attribs.description: How much damage the enemy can take before being defeated. 0 HP doesn't necessarily mean dead.

attribs.name: Movement Speed
attribs.description: How fast to reposition in the room.

attribs.name: Voltage
attribs.description: Determines electrical damage of an attack as well as how much energy is consumed.

attribs.name: XP
attribs.description: Dispatch rodents to gain experience — bigger ones yield more.

attribs.name: XP Worth
attribs.description: How much XP you gain from defeating the enemy.

events.name: Bite (To-Hit)
events.description:
Use teeth for a basic melee attack.

- **Damage:** If the attack hits then roll **Bite Dmg.**
- **Major Success:** (Nat 20) Enemy gets two damage rolls instead of one.
- **Major Failure:** Wild lunge—enemy cannot reposition on the next turn.

events.name: Bite Dmg
events.description: Roll, then apply that amount of damage.

events.name: Choose Mouse Action
events.description:
If there is a straightforward choice then there is no need to roll. At range, Baby mice typically wait, unless they're getting blasted. Adult mice use their ranged fur attack unless they have too little **HP.**

Otherwise, roll to determine what the mouse does next. If the outcome does not make sense for this encounter, then roll again. Mice aren't really fighters and often freeze (wait) at inopportune times.

events.name: Dodge
events.description: Attempt to evade a hit entirely or partially.

events.name: Fist Pummel (To-Hit)
events.description:
Close grasping digits and swing robotic arm forward. Basic melee attack.

- **Any Success:** If the attack hits then roll **Fist Pummel Dmg.**
- **Major Success:** (Nat 20) Two damage rolls instead of one.
- **Major Failure:** Consumes 2 **Energy** instead of 1.

After swinging, the enemy gets a turn.

events.name: Fist Pummel Dmg
events.description: Roll and apply the damage. If Enemy HP is reduced below 1 then do **XP Gain.**

events.name: Fresh Enemy
events.description:
A new enemy arrives. Whether the other one was dead or wounded doesn't matter anymore — it's gone now.

Run after the previous fight is over, or if you exited the area while the fight was in progress.

events.name: Ignite
events.description:
Shoot flames out of a nozzle that can reach up to 2 distance. Hard to dodge, but the flame doesn't always take.

- **Major Success:** 50% more damage
- Burning enemies must **Suffer DoT** for 3 turns
- **Any Failure:** lose energy, no damage

events.name: Level Up!
events.description:
Gaining a new level:
- increases **Energy** limit and sets current energy to full
- increases **Max HP** and sets **HP** to full
- **Force** and **Action Speed** increase every few levels

events.name: Maintenance Self
events.description: Use instead of returning to the **Maintenance Building,** either after the enemy is defeated or by travelling to a safe area.

events.name: Move Away
events.description:
Step out of melee range, repositioning to 5 tiles away.

- **Major Success:** (Nat 20) No loss of turn—the action is free.
- **Minor Success:** Move away and turn ends.
- **Minor Failure:** Disengage but allow an *Attack of Opportunity* (wait to apply distance).
- **Major Failure:** Still in melee range.

events.name: Perform Repairs
events.description: Higher damage is costlier to repair.

events.name: Project Fur (To-Hit)
events.description:
The creature goes wild and tears off some of its own corrupted fur to throw at you from distance.

- **Damage:** If the attack hits then roll **Project Fur Dmg.**
- **Major Failure:** More self-inflicted **HP** loss.
- This attack cannot crit—*Major Success* has no additional effect.

events.name: Project Fur Dmg
events.description: Damage applies to the target, not self-inflicted.

events.name: Refresh Boss
events.description: Run when you exit the area while the fight is in progress.

events.name: Resist Shock
events.description: Attempt to resist getting shocked. Is this necessary or can the attack event do the necessary roll?

events.name: Rush to Melee Dist
events.description:
Rush into melee range, repositioning to 1 tile away.

- **Success:** The action is free—no loss of turn.
- **Minor Failure:** Close gap but turn ends and (if robot) consumes 1 **Energy.**
- **Major Failure:** Fail to close gap.

events.name: Suffer DoT
events.description:
Damage over time due to burning or toxicity.
Run at the start of each turn even when the opponent does not attack.

events.name: Surge
events.description:
Gain bonus power and attack speed for 1 turn.

- Normally costs a turn to use. However, if you had just withdrawn to range and the enemy used their turn to follow, then it does not.
- Chance of causing a small penalty rather than a bonus.

events.name: Waste Away
events.description:
All enemies in this scenario are wasting away from toxins within. If they have under 5% HP remaining, or only 1 HP, then roll to see if they fully succumb.

Look in the modifiers section to verify that HP is low enough.

events.name: XP For Next Level
events.description: Shows the total XP needed to level up.

events.name: XP Gain
events.description:
- If the fight ended in victory, press *resolve* to gain experience equal to the enemy's level.
- If the calculation is at least 1 then you can level up.
- To fight the same kind of enemy again, find their **Fresh** event. Bosses however are unique.
